response.justification: While Sarah is clearly an excellent software engineer with strong fundamentals and diverse technical experience, she is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. Her C++ expertise ended around 2014-2015 (10+ years ago), and her career has pivoted entirely to web/full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript development. She has zero experience in capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking—none of which can be quickly acquired in a consulting engagement model. For a mid-senior position requiring 5-10 years of hands-on C++ and SQL Server/Oracle expertise, the ramp-up burden would be unjustifiable.
strengths: Exceptional software engineer with strong craftsmanship fundamentals and demonstrated ability to master diverse technologies and domains
Proven excellent communication skills through multiple workshops, conference talks, and teaching engagements
Broad technical depth across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems with experience in CI/CD and infrastructure
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and outdated (12+ years since last significant C++ work in 2014-2015); career has shifted entirely to JavaScript/TypeScript for the past decade
Completely lacks financial services, capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge with no visible exposure to this industry
SQL experience is primarily MySQL and MongoDB in web applications, not the SQL Server or Oracle expertise explicitly required for this role

key_factors.factor: Capital markets/fixed income domain knowledge absent
Strong software engineering fundamentals and learning ability
